# UC-22.29.16 · Technical measures adequacy review

## Description

Provides APAC privacy supervisory evidence for technical measures adequacy review aligned with PDPA SG; PIPL; K-ISMS.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for SG PDPA compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes edr and sourcetypes DefenderATP:Host, crowdstrike:hosts. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Maintain `apac_transfer_register.csv` and consent or DPIA lookups referenced in runbooks. (2) Map technical identifiers to records of processing activities with legal review. (3) Restrict indexes to regional privacy roles with least privilege. (4) Quarterly DPIA and consent linkage review with local counsel. Indexes required: `index=edr`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=DefenderATP:Host`, `sourcetype=crowdstrike:hosts`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (4055), Splunk Add-on for AWS (1876), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Windows Security Event Logs, cloud audit trails, DLP system outputs, consent management platforms, data classification tools, cross-border transfer logs, privacy impact assessments, breach notification records.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Maintain `apac_transfer_register.csv` and consent or DPIA lookups referenced in runbooks. (2) Map technical identifiers to records of processing activities with legal review. (3) Restrict indexes to regional privacy roles with least privilege. (4) Quarterly DPIA and consent linkage review with local counsel.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=edr sourcetype="crowdstrike:hosts" OR sourcetype="DefenderATP:Host" earliest=-24h
| stats latest(EncryptionStatus) as enc by hostname, os_version
| where enc!="Encrypted"
```

Understanding this SPL

**Technical measures adequacy review (PDPA SG; PIPL; K-ISMS)** — Provides APAC privacy supervisory evidence for technical measures adequacy review aligned with PDPA SG; PIPL; K-ISMS.

Documented **Data sources**: Windows Security Event Logs, cloud audit trails, DLP system outputs, consent management platforms, data classification tools, cross-border transfer logs, privacy impact assessments, breach notification records. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (4055), Splunk Add-on for AWS (1876), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: edr; **sourcetype**: crowdstrike:hosts, DefenderATP:Host. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=edr, sourcetype="crowdstrike:hosts", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by hostname, os_version** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• Filters the current rows with `where enc!="Encrypted"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the GRC system and the audit register, and with regional reporting cutoffs in your runbook. Document any expected regulatory reporting window or approved exception before escalating.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Stacked bar (jurisdiction x action), Table (open reviews), Single value (exports without mechanism).

## SPL

```spl
index=edr sourcetype="crowdstrike:hosts" OR sourcetype="DefenderATP:Host" earliest=-24h
| stats latest(EncryptionStatus) as enc by hostname, os_version
| where enc!="Encrypted"
```

## Visualization

Stacked bar (jurisdiction x action), Table (open reviews), Single value (exports without mechanism). Key fields: `enc`, `hostname`, `os_version`.
